description: Return the first index where the element exceeds threshold t, or -1 if none
code: lambda l,t:next((i for i,x in enumerate(l)if x>t),-1)

description: Return the minimum path sum from top-left to bottom-right of a grid, moving only right or down
Decode a run-length encoded list of [value, count] pairs into a flat list
code: lambda l:sum([[v]*n for v,n in l],[])

description: Return the list of run lengths of consecutive equal elements
Return the string with all vowels removed
Zip two lists into a list of pairs
Return the integer formed by concatenating the digits in the list
Return the Cartesian product of two lists as a list of [x, y] pairs
code: lambda a,b:[[x,y]for x in a for y in b]

description: Return n interleaved sublists (l[0::n], l[1::n], ...)
Check if matrix has a saddle point (min in row and max in col)
Return all unique pairs [a, b] from the list where a comes before b
code: lambda l:[[a,b]for i,a in enumerate(l)for b in l[i+1:]]

description: Return all subsets of the list whose elements sum to t
code: lambda l,t:[[*c]for r in range(len(l)+1)for c in __import__('itertools').combinations(l,r)if sum(c)==t]

description: Return a flattened list from a list of lists (one level deep)
Return all combinations of r elements from the list
Return the sorted list of unique elements from a 2D list
Count uppercase letters in a string
Return the second largest unique element from a list of integers
Return the length of the longest common subsequence of strings a and b
code: lambda a,b:(f:=lambda a,b:a and b and max(f(a[1:],b),f(a,b[1:]),a[0]==b[0]and-~f(a[1:],b[1:]))or 0)(a,b)

description: Return True if all elements in the list are equal, False otherwise
Decode a run-length encoded string where each run is formatted as count followed by character (e.g. '3a2b' -> 'aaabb')
Return all valid parentheses strings of n pairs in sorted order
code: lambda n:(f:=lambda s,o,c:o==c==n and[s]or(o<n and f(s+'(',o+1,c)or[])+(c<o and f(s+')',o,c+1)or[]))('',0,0)

description: Maximum nesting depth of nested list
Return a sorted list of [value, count] pairs for each unique element
Return run-length encoding of the string, omitting counts of 1 (e.g. 'aabc' -> 'a2bc')
Return the string formed by the list of ASCII codes
Return a list of only the even numbers from the input list
code: lambda l:[x for x in l if~x%2]

description: Return the longest common prefix string among all strings in the list
code: lambda l:''.join(a for a,*b in zip(*l)if len({a,*b})<2)

description: Return the product of all numbers in the list
Return the list flattened to depth d
code: lambda l,d:(f:=lambda a,n:sum([(f(i,n-1)if n*(type(i)==list)else[i])for i in a],[]))(l,d)

description: Return all binary strings of length n in order
Return the n x n identity matrix as a list of lists
Return the maximum subarray sum (Kadane's algorithm)
Return every nth element of the list (starting from index 0)
Return a list of [element, running_sum] pairs
Return the string with the first and last characters swapped
Return the missing number from a list containing all integers from 1 to n+1 except one
Return the last n elements of a list
Return the list of all divisors of a positive integer n
code: lambda n:[i for i in range(1,n+1)if n%i<1]

description: Return the sorted list of elements common to both lists
Return the first equilibrium index (where left sum equals right sum), or -1 if none
code: lambda l:next((i for i in range(len(l))if sum(l[:i])==sum(l[i+1:])),-1)
